title: acer miyabei f. shibatae
intro: Acer miyabei f. shibatae, also known as the Miyabe maple or Shibata's maple, is a deciduous tree that belongs to the family Sapindaceae, specifically a variant of the Miyabe maple tree (Acer miyabei). Traditionally, the bark of this tree has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure and act as an anti-inflammatory. The health benefits of Acer miyabei f. shibatae include its potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, improve blood circulation, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the bark is typically dried and powdered for use in teas, capsules, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
